page: Amazon Web Services x8i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance across multiple domains. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions), contribute to top-tier scores in Passmark extended instructions and Geekbench ray tracing. Multi-threaded performance is strong, as demonstrated by top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached small-block read speeds reaching top-tier levels of 7680.48 GB/sec, while large-block reads and writes maintain strong performance.

This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that require substantial RAM and high-speed memory access without local storage requirements. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, as evidenced by top-tier Redis SET operations exceeding 171 million operations per second and strong Passmark database performance. Additionally, the inclusion of AMX instructions enables top-tier prompt processing speeds for medium-sized (7B) LLM inference, making it viable for CPU-based machine learning tasks alongside traditional enterprise applications and data-intensive processing.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, delivering 16.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and cached memory read bandwidth. Advanced instruction support, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enables top-tier performance in extended instructions and prompt processing for medium-sized LLM inference. This instance is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis deployments, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 1536 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier performance for in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 1.5 TB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity, memory-optimized server featuring advanced Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for demanding in-memory database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C at 3.9 GHz
1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 16.0 GB per core
Top-tier cached memory bandwidth and multi-core processing capabilities
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated mathematical and AI workloads
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis, and heavy data processing
